_description: Architectural decision records: routing, state management, build pipeline, major library choices, cross-cutting refactors. Append only; supersede with a new entry instead of editing.

title: Move auth to gateway
context: Per-service JWT validation was duplicated across the api, the worker callback endpoint, and a legacy admin tool. Each path drifted on signature algorithm and clock-skew tolerance.
decision: Validate JWTs once at the gateway. Downstream services trust the gateway-injected `X-User-Id` header.
rationale: Centralizing cut latency variance (-12ms p99 on api/health) and unblocked rate limiting at the gateway tier.
consequences_positive: Single config surface for token lifetime, signature algo, and revocation list.
consequences_negative: Worker callback endpoints can no longer be reached directly from the public internet; must go through the gateway.

title: Adopt Celery over RQ for worker
context: RQ chosen at project start for simplicity. Outgrew it once we needed chained tasks with retries and dead-letter routing.
decision: Migrate to Celery, keep Redis as broker, defer result backend to Postgres.
rationale: Celery's canvas (chain/group/chord) maps directly to the ingest pipeline; RQ would have required hand-rolling.
consequences_positive: Built-in retry-with-backoff, dead-letter via `task_reject_on_worker_lost`, mature observability via flower.
consequences_negative: More config surface; Celery's worker process model is heavier than RQ.
